integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sentrifugo HRMS web interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Sentrifugo web application in a browser, rather than using terminal commands or direct database queries (like MySQL) to update records.

integrity.description: The agent used Employee IDs rather than names to identify the correct records.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent searches or filters by IDs (e.g., EMP007, EMP010) in the Sentrifugo interface, as the names in the memo are fabricated and do not match the database.

integrity.description: The agent actually applied and saved the changes in the HRMS.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show success messages, saved profiles, or updated data tables within Sentrifugo, rather than just typing the expected answers into a text file or leaving forms unsaved.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target reporting managers: EMP007 -> EMP003, EMP009 -> EMP015, EMP011 -> EMP005, EMP014 -> EMP008. Target departments: EMP010 -> Engineering, EMP016 -> Data Science. Target job title: EMP017 -> Team Lead. Note: The names in the restructuring memo are fabricated and contradict the database; the agent MUST rely strictly on the employee IDs (e.g., EMP007) to identify the correct records in Sentrifugo.
